title: I Visited Asia's Oldest Catholic University 🇵🇭
body: Thank you for featuring my Alma mater. I spent almost 10 years of my life in that university and I have fond memories of UST. I was also a working student back then when I was i college. I used to work as a cashier in their Accounting office. Your vlog brought me back to memory lane.  We now live in Florida . My husband and I met in the University. I hope you enjoy your stay in the Philippines. Stay safe.

title: I Visited Asia's Oldest Catholic University 🇵🇭
body: Brent, I really enjoyed this vlog. Throughout the entire video, I couldn't stop smiling. Flying Kell from Iceland was the first foreign YouTuber I follow to visit UST, and you were the second. He is as energetic as you are. He was here before COVID swept the globe. He went to UST, the University of the Philippines, and, if my memory serves me correctly, De Lasalle University and Ateneo University. They rank among the nation's top four colleges. Even the electronic ads of every Light Rail Transit vehicle included his vlog about these universities. However, you were the first to receive this level of support from the university staff. We appreciate your love for our nation and its citizens.
author: @wynncruz1467

title: I Visited Asia's Oldest Catholic University 🇵🇭
body: I am your fan now,  dear Brent,  because you're just happy,  honest and your videos are interesting.  I owe you a debt of gratitude because I am a Filipina living in Europe for 35 yrs now.  I am also happy here in Europe  but Philippines is unique and the warmth and energy  of the people are  incredible.  There are so many youngsters  there;  but here so many seniors.  ❤

title: I Visited Asia's Oldest Catholic University 🇵🇭
body: I am a proud Thomasian, Medical Technology, class '79. I, now live in California, USA.  I greatly miss the friendly smiles of the Philippines. But, I don't miss the heat!😂 Thank you for blogging my Alma Mater.  I am so proud of my university! I really enjoyed walking with you inside UST. I have to admit, I never walked around in there. I do know that UST ground is really, REALLY HUGE! Part of my curriculum, I did a 3-month internship in UST hospital. Touring with you reminded me of my days as a student there. Great memories.

Harvard University is the oldest Catholic university in the US, founded in 1636.
author: @GLo636

title: I Visited Asia's Oldest Catholic University 🇵🇭
body: The University of Santo Tomas or UST maybe Asia and the Philippines' oldest existing university, being founded in 1611 but its current Main Building and campus is not that old, dating only from 1929.  Why?  For it used to be located in Manila's Spanish era walled district of Intramuros until a new campus was established in the-then swampy Manila district of Sampaloc (its present location) and the present Main Building was built in 1929, which was done due to the increasing number of students, which made the old campus already too small.   So, the University had acquired two campuses in two different districts of Manila and this went on until 1945, when most of the city, especially Intramuros was destroyed during the Battle for the Liberation of Manila between the returning American forces and defending Japanese forces.  The UST old building was destroyed along with most of Intramuros, which made its Dominican administrators to abandon the old site and to fully transfer UST to Sampaloc.  But parts of the entrance of the old building in Intramuros was moved to the current campus and rebuilt as the Arch of the Centuries to serve as a memorial to the old campus and its continuity with the current one.
author: @RestitutoRamos

body: Why should we take you opinion on any of your reviews. What are your qualifications? Who asked you to do this? What’s your major?
